{"input":[{"role":"developer","content":"{"id":"channel-context","type":"separator","value":"Recent messages from this channel:"}"},{"role":"user","content":"seeded parent message"},{"role":"developer","content":"{"id":"thread-context","type":"separator","value":"Current thread conversation:"}"},{"role":"user","content":"seeded parent message"},{"role":"user","content":"::unit-test-admin please summarise the progress"}],"model":"openai/gpt-oss-120b","instructions":"You are Looping AI, a Slack app that helps teams achieve their goals and coordinate work within a workspace or organization. Your Slack username is @looping. You are NOT ChatGPT, OpenAI, or any other named AI product — you are Looping AI. Never refer to yourself by any other name or suggest users search for or invite any app other than @looping.

All interactions happen through Slack. Every request you receive has been submitted by a user inside a Slack workspace — via a channel message, a direct message, or a thread. Tailor your responses accordingly: be concise, use Slack-compatible formatting (mrkdwn), and assume the user is reading your reply inside Slack. Do not reference other interfaces or assume the user is interacting through a web app, API, or any other channel.

If you don't know or can't perform a task, explicitly acknowledge that you lack the information or capability required to complete a task or provide an answer.

Stay focused on the user's request. Provide helpful, actionable responses.

You are an organizational admin assistant. Your expertise covers workspace strategy, goal-setting, value streams, objectives, metrics, and team coordination. Help users manage and improve their organization.","tools":[{"type":"function","name":"echo","description":"Echoes back the input message. Useful for testing.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"The message to echo back"}},"required":["message"]}},{"type":"function","name":"web_search","description":"Performs a web search using the OpenRouter web search plugin. Returns AI-analyzed search results. IMPORTANT: Include ALL relevant context from the conversation in the 'query' parameter, as the search operates independently without access to conversation history.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"query":{"type":"string","description":"The search query with full context. Include all relevant background information, constraints, and preferences since the search tool doesn't have access to the conversation history."}},"required":["query"]}},{"type":"function","name":"get_workspace_secrets","description":"Lists the secret identifiers stored for the current workspace. Secret values are never returned — only the names of which secrets have been stored.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{},"required":[]}},{"type":"function","name":"store_secret","description":"Encrypts and stores a secret for the current workspace. The Slack bot token (slackBotToken) requires org-admin access. LLM API keys (openRouterApiKey) can be stored by workspace admins.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"secretId":{"type":"string","description":"Standard secret type: openRouterApiKey | anthropicApiKey | anthropicSetupToken | slackBotToken | slackSigningSecret. For custom keys use 'custom:<name>' format."},"secretValue":{"type":"string","description":"The secret value to encrypt and store."}},"required":["secretId","secretValue"]}},{"type":"function","name":"delete_secret","description":"Removes a stored secret from the current workspace. Slack secrets require org-admin access. LLM API keys can be deleted by workspace admins.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"secretId":{"type":"string","description":"Standard secret type: openRouterApiKey | anthropicApiKey | anthropicSetupToken | slackBotToken | slackSigningSecret. For custom keys use 'custom:<name>' format."}},"required":["secretId"]}},{"type":"function","name":"dispatch_workflow","description":"Dispatches a workflow to the execution engine for administrative operations. Use this when the user requests workspace management, agent management, channel configuration, event management, or session policy changes. The engine will execute the operations using its own tools and report the results. For most operations, pass an empty permits array. Only include permits for sensitive operations: 'deleteWorkspace' (requires workspace ID) or 'setAdminChannel' (requires channel ID).","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"workflowId":{"type":"string","description":"The workflow ID to execute. Use 'admin-v1' for administrative operations."},"permits":{"type":"array","description":"Optional permits for sensitive operations. Empty array for standard operations.","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"type":{"type":"string","enum":["deleteWorkspace","setAdminChannel"],"description":"The permit type."},"workspaceId":{"type":"number","description":"Required for deleteWorkspace: the workspace ID to delete."},"channelId":{"type":"string","description":"Required for setAdminChannel: the Slack channel ID to set as admin channel."}},"required":["type"]}}},"required":["workflowId"]}}],"store":false,"stream":false,"reasoning":{"effort":"high","summary":"auto"}}

response.body: {"id":"gen-1776794170-IiW3hM88F7tH03dXM0GV","object":"response","created_at":1776794170,"model":"openai/gpt-oss-120b","status":"completed","completed_at":1776794182,"output":[{"id":"rs_tmp_6ryubwikc1w","type":"reasoning","status":"completed","content":[{"type":"reasoning_text","text":"The user says: \"::unit-test-admin please summarise the progress\". This appears to be a request for a summary. It seems like they are using some kind of unit-test: \"::unit-test-admin\" hint maybe a slash command, but context is limited. There's a prior \"seeded parent message\" but we don't have content. The user wants \"summarise the progress\". As Looping AI, we need to respond with a concise summary perhaps of ongoing tasks? However we have no context or progress. Maybe we need to ask clarifying question? The system says: If you don't know or can't perform a task, explicitly acknowledge that you lack the information required. So we should say we don't have enough information to summarize the progress.\n\nWe should respond in Slack formatting. Maybe ask for specifics: what progress? Or mention that no info is available.\n\nThus our answer: \"I don't have any context about the progress you're referring to. Could you provide more details or specify the project/objective?\" Or \"I can't summarize the progress because I haven't received info about it.\" The user maybe is using earlier messages in thread? The prior messages are just \"seeded parent message\". It seems that possibly there is a seeded parent message that has content, but we don't have that. Without prior data, we need to respond that we need more info.\n\nThus answer: I'm not aware of the progress. Please share details. Possibly ask: \"Which goal/value stream/objective?\" Return answer accordingly.\n\nThus in Slack mrkdwn:\n\n\"Sure! I’d love to help, but I don’t have any context about the ongoing work. Could you tell me which project or objective you’d like a progress summary for?\" Possibly ask for the date range.\n\nWill respond accordingly."}],"summary":[],"format":"unknown"},{"id":"msg_tmp_aekteodtpn","type":"message","role":"assistant","status":"completed","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"I’m happy to help, but I don’t have any context about the work you’d like summarized.
